channel.title: Clay Liang — Blog
channel.description: Data, markets, and the space between models and decisions.

item.title: My First DCF: Valuing ASML as a Leiden Student
item.description: ASML is 45 minutes from my university. I used my own DCF calculator to value it from scratch — three scenarios, real numbers, and every place I was unsure.

item.title: The Bimodal Reality of CS Careers
item.description: CS has two career tracks: research scientist and infrastructure engineer. The middle is hollow.

item.title: Notes on Building a Databricks Pipeline That Doesn't Fall Over
item.description: Practical things I learned processing 10TB+ of aviation data — partition strategies, avoiding collect(), and why your notebook that works at 1GB will betray you at 100GB.

item.title: What an Aircraft Factory Taught Me About Data
item.description: I went to Airbus expecting to build models. Instead I spent most of my time cleaning SQL queries, arguing about chart colours, and learning that the hardest part of data work has nothing to do with data.

item.title: Why I Read Annual Reports as a Data Person
item.description: Everyone says "data-driven." But most data work never touches the documents where real decisions get recorded.

item.title: From Loss Functions to Decisions: Why I'm Moving Toward Finance
item.description: I spent two years optimising models and one year optimising slide decks. The slide decks changed more outcomes.
